title: Trichomanes angustifrons
intro: Trichomanes angustifrons is a type of fern, specifically a species of the family Hymenophyllaceae, known for its delicate fronds and unique growth habits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Trichomanes angustifrons include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Trichomanes angustifrons is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Trichomanes angustifrons is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family consists of about 700 species of delicate, often epiphytic or lithophytic ferns. Trichomanes angustifrons is part of the genus Trichomanes, which is characterized by its long-stemmed and usually solitary fronds. The classification of Trichomanes angustifrons places it as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns and their allies. The specific classification of Trichomanes angustifrons can be represented as Trichomanes angustifrons (Bory) Bosch.
